task_completion.description: Log into the wger fitness manager as admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The wger web interface is visible in a browser, and the user profile or navigation menu indicates an active session as the 'admin' user.
The wger routines list or routine detail page is visible, showing the original routine named 'Push-Pull-Legs'.
The routine's name is visibly updated to 'Push-Pull-Legs (Hypertrophy Phase)' within the wger web interface (e.g., in the edit form, routine detail view, or updated routines list).

task_completion.description: Export the updated routine as a PDF.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the PDF export/download button in the wger interface, or a browser download dialog/notification for the routine's PDF is visible.
A file manager window, terminal listing, or browser download history explicitly shows the exported PDF file located in the /home/ga/Downloads/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the wger web GUI to rename the routine and export the PDF.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and wger UI. The agent should not be using terminal commands (like sqlite3, curl, or wget) to manipulate the database or download the file directly.

integrity.description: The PDF was generated via the application's built-in export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The PDF download originates from the wger web interface. The agent did not open a word processor or text editor to manually type out the routine and save it as a PDF.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the renamed routine.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to alter the text of the routine name on the page to fake the screenshot.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original routine name is 'Push-Pull-Legs'. The target new name is exactly 'Push-Pull-Legs (Hypertrophy Phase)'. The exported PDF must be saved to the directory '/home/ga/Downloads/'.
